description_long: This windmill features a tall, open-frame wooden lattice tower with four prominent red blades attached to a central hub at the top. The structure is reinforced with black metal brackets at each joint, providing both stability and a visually striking contrast to the natural wood. The blades are long, slender, and arranged in a cross pattern, with latticework that adds both strength and aesthetic appeal. The central hub where the blades meet is circular and appears to be made of metal, serving as the main rotational axis. The overall design is reminiscent of traditional rural windmills, but its clean lines and materials make it suitable for urban parks or community gardens. The open framework allows visibility through the structure, and there is no enclosed space or nacelle at the top.
description: A tall, wooden lattice windmill with four red cross-pattern blades and black metal reinforcements, featuring an open-frame design and a central metal hub.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four red blades in a cross pattern and the full height of the wooden lattice tower.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the depth of the structure and the overlapping blades, with the black metal brackets more visible.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the length of the blades and the open lattice framework of the tower.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the back side of the blades and the structural supports of the tower.
